enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief update on a vlog day, sharing that he worked until 4am on a tracing project recreating ancient Roman structures (Flaminian Circus, Pompeii Amphitheater, temples) for a 3D visualization. He's excited about the creative possibilities for virtual tours and movies, and mentions listening to Mike Duncan's history podcast while working on a Rome timeline.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: extremely excited about the final product and its possibilities for virtual tours and movies
recommends it highly for its memorable, well-emphasized storytelling about Rome

stances.topic: which YouTube channel to use for the project
stances.position: undecided between creating a new channel or using his existing one

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle worked until about 4:15 in the morning on November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle got up and started working again from 5 AM to noon on November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle completed tracing the Flaminian Circus and Pompeii Amphitheater on November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle also traced some other temples and structures on November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle estimates that half of his tracing work is done as of November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has completed tracing most of the big buildings as of November 28th, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is excited to see the final 3D product of his tracing work because the possibilities are immense.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to create movies that transition from one room to another and offer tours of buildings using his tracing work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is undecided whether to showcase his tracing work on a new YouTube channel or his existing one.

claims.subject: Mike Duncan
claims.claim: Kyle recommends Mike Duncan's podcast for its ability to help listeners remember important historical events.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to listen to Mike Duncan's podcast while updating his website timeline for Rome.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log. Today's Tuesday. The V-Log's for Monday, November 28th.
It's noon now. I was working until about 4.15 in the morning, and then I got up and I'm
Working, so that's 5 to noon, 7.5 hours. From work to work. Not from sleep, but from work
To work. So, yesterday I did the rest of the Flaminian Amphitheater, and then I did a
Lot of the Pompeii Amphitheater. No, no, Flaminian Circus, and then Pompeii Amphitheater, and
Then some other temples and stuff. As far as tracing goes, I'm trucking along. Looks like
Half of it's done. I'm done with most of the big buildings. And then they 3D-ify it.
I'm, like, freaking out, dude. I've never been as, like, as eager to see the final product
As this, as I've been to see this, because the possibilities are immense. I want to make,
Like, movies going from somebody's room to somebody's other room, and all kinds of tours,
Inside tours, outside tours of all the buildings. The only thing I can't decide is, should I
Do it with a new YouTube channel or with my YouTube channel?
Uh, so, uh, yeah, I'm totally rested now. Oh, I'll just listen to the Mike Duncan. I think I have
20 more episodes of Mike Duncan. If anybody, you should check out Mike Duncan's podcast. It's really
Good, because, about this through a room, because, um, he's good at stressing and, you know, talking in a
Way that helps you remember, like, where the important, uh, the important stuff happens. So, it'll be good
To listen to when I, uh, touch up my website. I mean, my timeline, just for Rome. So, uh, anyway, nothing
Really changed. Everything's the same. Trucking along. All right. Peace out.
